description: A social psychology lab has collected Implicit Association Test (IAT) data stored at ~/pebl/data/iat_data.csv, containing columns for participant, block, trial, rt_ms, and correct. Your task is to compute the standard Greenwald et al. (2003) D-score for each participant and identify automated/bot responders. Using data from blocks 3, 4, 6, and 7, exclude participants if more than 10% of their trials across those blocks have an rt_ms below 300 ms. For valid participants, calculate the D-score by averaging the quotients of the mean differences (Block 6 minus Block 3; Block 7 minus Block 4) divided by the pooled sample standard deviations of the corresponding blocks (3 and 6; 4 and 7). Save the results to ~/pebl/analysis/iat_report.json as a JSON object containing a 'participants' list with 'id' and 'd_score' (or 'excluded': true with a 'reason' string) and a 'group_mean_d_score' field for the overall mean of valid participants.
